subject: Re: Fw: Update
body:
refernce's of course ,,but thats it

--- Gmax <gmax1@mindspring.com> wrote:

> How do you want me to reply - was planning on saying
> yes of course will given reference etc..be v
> nice..no surprise here...your thoughts
> G
> 
> -----Original Message-----
> From: Jen Doyle <jennifercdoyle@yahoo.com>
> Date: Fri, 29 Sep 2006 09:23:48 
> To:gmax1@mindspring.com, jeeproject@yahoo.com
> Subject: Update
> 
> Dear Jeffrey and Ghislaine, 
>   
> I am writing because as promised, I want to always
> keep you apprised of what's going on, in my life. 
> That said, it is with great regret that I must note
> that I was let go from from my position in Mr.
> Diller's office, last Wednesday.  It is my
> understadning that there is, in fact, no position
> available.  The young woman who I was supposed to
> replace never left during my almost 3 months there,
> and I will offer that it was quite clear that she
> didn't seem ready to leave, as immediately as 1
> month into my time, there.  I don't think it was a
> malicious move on anyone's part, however, her
> indecision has certainly caused me quite an expense.
> 
>   
> ... It is thus, a time of interviewing, for me,
> again and as such, I am also writing to ask if you
> will once again be a reference to me.  I imagine
> that your opinion of my work ethic has not changed
> and I hope you won't mind offering your opinion of
> me, again, to new, potential employers.  You have my
> sincere gratitude for your help.   
>   
> Furthermore, I wanted to let you know how much I
> appreciated my time in your employment.  I have
> always been mindful of how good you both were to me,
> but this fact resonates that much more with me, now
> that I've taken a step outside.  While I certainly
> would have stuck the job out, in Mr. Diller's
> office, had I the opportunity, I will honestly say
> that my experience there, wasn't even a quarter of
> the pleasure, I took in working for you.  I guess
> hindsight is always 20/20, but I wanted to at least
> let you know how much my time in your employment
> meant and still means to me - and that given the
> opportunity, I would coming running back.   
>   
> Thank you again for all that you did for me, were to
> me and are to me... 
> Jen 
>   
>

body_html:
refernce's of course ,,but thats it

--- Gmax gmax1@mindspring.com wrote:

How do you want me to reply - was planning on saying
yes of course will given reference etc..be v
nice..no surprise here...your thoughts
G

-----Original Message-----
From: Jen Doyle jennifercdoyle@yahoo.com
Date: Fri, 29 Sep 2006 09:23:48
To:gmax1@mindspring.com, jeeproject@yahoo.com
Subject: Update

Dear Jeffrey and Ghislaine,

I am writing because as promised, I want to always
keep you apprised of what's going on, in my life.
That said, it is with great regret that I must note
that I was let go from from my position in Mr.
Diller's office, last Wednesday. It is my
understadning that there is, in fact, no position
available. The young woman who I was supposed to
replace never left during my almost 3 months there,
and I will offer that it was quite clear that she
didn't seem ready to leave, as immediately as 1
month into my time, there. I don't think it was a
malicious move on anyone's part, however, her
indecision has certainly caused me quite an expense.

... It is thus, a time of interviewing, for me,
again and as such, I am also writing to ask if you
will once again be a reference to me. I imagine
that your opinion of my work ethic has not changed
and I hope you won't mind offering your opinion of
me, again, to new, potential employers. You have my
sincere gratitude for your help.

Furthermore, I wanted to let you know how much I
appreciated my time in your employment. I have
always been mindful of how good you both were to me,
but this fact resonates that much more with me, now
that I've taken a step outside. While I certainly
would have stuck the job out, in Mr. Diller's
office, had I the opportunity, I will honestly say
that my experience there, wasn't even a quarter of
the pleasure, I took in working for you. I guess
hindsight is always 20/20, but I wanted to at least
let you know how much my time in your employment
meant and still means to me - and that given the
opportunity, I would coming running back.

Thank you again for all that you did for me, were to
me and are to me...
Jen
